title: An Hymn To Juno.
content:
Stately goddess, do thou please,
    Who are chief at marriages,
    But to dress the bridal bed
    When my love and I shall wed;
    And a peacock proud shall be
    Offered up by us to thee.
meaning:
"An Hymn To Juno." is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
